title: Soldier subagent report
description: Structured return contract for every Soldier dispatched per rules/subagent-orchestration.md. The Agent tool returns a single message; the contract is that the Soldier's FINAL message is a JSON object matching this schema. The Captain validates via scripts/lib/schema.mjs and halts (per rules/ambiguity-halt.md) on validation failure rather than silently retrying.

status.description: Terminal status. `done` means the Soldier completed the task; `failed` means it could not proceed (briefing was wrong, scope was impossible, preflight tripped); `partial` means it completed some of the task and stopped at ambiguity or a blocker it could not classify.

summary.description: Plain-language description of what the Soldier did. The Captain reads this first; everything else is for acting on the result. Under 2,400 chars (roughly one screen) so the Captain doesn't lose context to a long-winded report.

artefacts.description: Filesystem paths the Soldier read, wrote, or cited. Implementer briefings REQUIRE this to be non-empty and contain every written file. Explorers may return an empty array when they scanned via Grep / Glob only. Reviewers may return empty when they worked from a supplied diff. Paths may be absolute or project-relative; the Captain verifies their existence before acting on the report.

blockers.description: Short strings naming the things the Soldier could not resolve. Populated ONLY when `status` is `partial` or `failed`. Each entry is a sentence the Captain can paste directly into a user-facing halt per rules/ambiguity-halt.md.

nextStep.description: Optional single sentence naming the action the Captain should consider next (for example 'spawn an Implementer Soldier for findings 1, 3, 5'). Advisory only; the Captain decides.
